features.summary: Deere reported strong Q2 2021 results with net sales up 30% to $12.1B and equipment operations margin of 19.5%. Production and Precision Ag segment saw 35% sales growth with 22% operating margin, driven by higher volumes and 9 points of positive price realization. Small Ag and Turf grew 30% with 19% margins. Construction and Forestry increased 36% with 15.9% margins. Order books are complete through fiscal 2021 and extending into 2022 for large tractors, indicating strong demand momentum.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year net income guidance to $5.3-5.7B and expects continued strong performance into 2022. They anticipate Production and Precision Ag sales up 25-30% for fiscal 2021 with 20-21% operating margins. The company sees a multi-year equipment cycle driven by aged fleet, low inventory levels, and strong agricultural fundamentals. Management is optimistic about precision agriculture adoption and performance upgrade opportunities across the installed base.
features.risks: Supply chain disruptions remain the primary concern, with semiconductor shortages posing significant production risks. The company faces $1B in material and freight cost inflation, with 75% hitting in the second half. Labor shortages across suppliers and logistics providers are constraining production capacity. COVID-related disruptions continue affecting foreign operations, particularly in India. Raw material price increases, especially steel, and premium freight utilization are pressuring margins.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals with grain consumption outpacing supply and 30% increase in U.S. crop cash receipts are driving demand. The North American fleet is at its oldest age in two decades while inventory levels remain at historic lows. Precision agriculture technology adoption is accelerating, with new products like See & Spray and ExactRate launching. Performance upgrade opportunities across the installed base provide additional growth avenues beyond new equipment sales.
